executive_summary.main_recommendation: AI consumption keeps rising: 7959.9 MT in 2024-10 to 10351 MT in 2025-03 (+30%), peaking at 10351 MT. Cut waste first: Clara Chevalier, Adrien Ivanova, Chloe Leroy burn too many tokens for their delivery; cap usage, review prompts, and retrain on smaller context. Scale what works: Zoe Laurent, Remy Dupuis convert tokens into delivery efficiently; have them coach peers. Fix under-adoption: Simon Martin, Quentin Morin, Lucas Renard use little AI and deliver little; run enablement and track story points plus tickets next month.

employee_metrics.name: Zoe Laurent
employee_metrics.recommendation: Zoe Laurent sets the best example to replicate: 62 story points is rank #1 and 456666666 tokens is below the team median of 662777777. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Zoe is at 7365591 versus the team median of 17158730, with 1.56 days to completion versus 2.56 days median. Praise this behavior and have Zoe coach peers on prompt discipline, smaller context, and task selection that should be copied. Next month, watch tokens per story point.

employee_metrics.name: Remy Dupuis
employee_metrics.recommendation: Remy Dupuis is a strong pattern to replicate: 45 story points is above the team median of 40 while 564555555 tokens is below the team median of 662777777. Story points per million tokens means how much delivery came from each million tokens; higher is better, and Remy is at 0.08 versus the team median of 0.06, with 11 tickets resolved versus 7 median. Praise this usage and ask Remy to mentor peers on the prompt and workflow habits behind this result. Next month, watch story points per million tokens.

employee_metrics.name: Alexandre Diallo
employee_metrics.recommendation: Alexandre Diallo is close to the team middle: 33 story points is below the median of 40, while 557555555 tokens is below the median of 662777777. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Alexandre is at 16895623, almost the same as the team median of 17158730, with tickets resolved matching the median at 7. Monitor rather than cut usage, and retrain on the efficient-user playbook if token use rises without more delivery. Next month, watch story points.

employee_metrics.name: Alix Mbaye
employee_metrics.recommendation: Alix Mbaye is using AI at a balanced level: 42 story points is slightly above the team median of 40, and 720666666 tokens is slightly above the median of 662777777. Tokens per story point is exactly 17158730, which matches the team median, while 10 tickets resolved is above the median of 7 and completion time is faster at 2.3 days versus 2.56 days. Monitor this pattern and replicate efficient-user habits only if higher token use stops translating into more delivery. Next month, watch tokens per story point.

employee_metrics.name: Fatima Muller
employee_metrics.recommendation: Fatima Muller is delivering strong results with solid token use: 52 story points is well above the team median of 40, while 688888888 tokens is only slightly above the median of 662777777. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Fatima is at 13247863 versus the team median of 17158730, with 10 tickets resolved versus 7 median. Monitor and replicate the workflow if this pattern holds, because this is a good example of getting more delivery without a large token increase. Next month, watch tokens per story point.

employee_metrics.name: Leila Park
employee_metrics.recommendation: Leila Park is slightly better than the team middle: 42 story points is above the median of 40, and token use at 662777777 is exactly at the team median. Tokens per story point is 15780423 versus the team median of 17158730, which means slightly fewer tokens were needed for each story point, and completion time is faster at 2 days versus 2.56 days. Monitor this pattern and replicate the workflow if the same balance of delivery and token use continues. Next month, watch tokens per story point.

employee_metrics.name: Nina Traore
employee_metrics.recommendation: Nina Traore is using more AI than the team median without matching delivery: 760000000 tokens is above the median of 662777777, but 34 story points is below the median of 40. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Nina is at 22352941 versus the team median of 17158730, while completion time is slower at 3 days versus 2.56 days. Retrain on smaller context and tighter prompt use, and monitor closely because cost should be controlled when higher token use does not produce more delivery. Next month, watch tokens per story point.

employee_metrics.name: Victor Durand
employee_metrics.recommendation: Victor Durand is near the team median but not clearly efficient: 40 story points matches the team median, while 702888888 tokens is above the median of 662777777. Tokens per story point is 17572222 versus the team median of 17158730, so slightly more tokens were needed for each story point, and tickets resolved at 6 is below the median of 7. Monitor this pattern and retrain if token use increases again without more story points or tickets. Next month, watch tickets_resolved.

employee_metrics.name: Lucas Renard
employee_metrics.recommendation: Lucas Renard looks under-adopted more than wasteful: 550666666 tokens is below the team median of 662777777, and 28 story points is also below the median of 40. Story points per million tokens means how much delivery came from each million tokens; higher is better, and Lucas is at 0.05 versus the team median of 0.06, with slower completion at 3.83 days versus 2.56 days. Run adoption coaching rather than budget cuts, because better use of AI may create more value than reducing already-below-median usage. Next month, watch story_points.

employee_metrics.name: Quentin Morin
employee_metrics.recommendation: Quentin Morin is a clear low-adoption case: 172444444 tokens is far below the team median of 662777777, and 19 story points is also far below the median of 40. Even though tokens per story point is strong at 9076023 versus the team median of 17158730, total delivery remains low with 5 tickets resolved versus 7 median, so the issue is limited use rather than waste. Run adoption coaching and expand use on suitable tasks, because enablement is more likely to create value here than cutting an already small budget. Next month, watch tickets_resolved.

employee_metrics.name: Simon Martin
employee_metrics.recommendation: Simon Martin is also under-adopted, not wasteful: 107222222 tokens is the lowest on the team and far below the median of 662777777, while 14 story points is also the lowest and below the median of 40. Story points per million tokens means how much delivery came from each million tokens; higher is better, and Simon is at 0.13 versus the team median of 0.06, so the token use that does happen is efficient, but total output is still low at 3 tickets resolved versus 7 median. Run adoption coaching instead of budget cuts, because broader use on more tasks may lift delivery without needing much extra spend. Next month, watch story_points.

employee_metrics.name: Julien Bourgeois
employee_metrics.recommendation: Julien Bourgeois should not receive more AI usage yet: 597111111 tokens is only below the team median of 662777777, but story points are just 16 versus the team median of 40. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Julien is at 37319444 versus the team median of 17158730, with only 3 tickets resolved and 3.33 days to completion versus 2.56 days median. Retrain on task breakdown and add closer monitoring before allowing heavier AI use, because current spend is not turning into enough delivery. Next month, watch tokens per story point.

employee_metrics.name: Adrien Ivanova
employee_metrics.recommendation: Adrien Ivanova is a clear cost-control priority: 1401777777 tokens is the highest on the team and far above the median of 662777777, while 46 story points is only modestly above the median of 40. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Adrien is at 30473430 versus the team median of 17158730, so cost should be reduced because much more token spend is buying only a limited delivery gain. Cap usage, review prompt logs, and retrain on smaller context before approving any increase. Next month, watch tokens_used.

employee_metrics.name: Chloe Leroy
employee_metrics.recommendation: Chloe Leroy is overspending relative to delivery: 1227222222 tokens is far above the team median of 662777777, while 46 story points is only above the median of 40 by a small amount. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Chloe is at 26678744 versus the team median of 17158730, so cost should be controlled because token spend is rising faster than delivery. Reduce or cap usage, review prompt patterns, and retrain on smaller context and tighter task selection. Next month, watch tokens per story point.

employee_metrics.name: Clara Chevalier
employee_metrics.recommendation: Clara Chevalier is the strongest case for reducing waste: 1180555555 tokens is far above the team median of 662777777, but story points are only 31 versus the team median of 40. Tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point; lower is better, and Clara is at 38082437 versus the team median of 17158730, which explains why cost should be reduced and controlled. Cap usage, retrain on prompt discipline and smaller context, and monitor closely before allowing more AI spend. Next month, watch tokens per story point.
